principle: Scene/layout approval is separate from product-consistency approval. Only images passing both can enter the reusable library.

keep_patterns.L081: Clean product source, simple drawer/frame structure, compatible kitchen/patio sideboard scenes, no competing rack-like style object; product stayed supported and recognizable.
keep_patterns.L086: Living room sideboard and modern kitchen scenes matched real use; product remained front-facing/counter-supported and style references did not strongly compete with the two-drawer rack structure.
keep_patterns.L088: The kept luxury dining image used the black/high-contrast source and inset; rods and basket silhouette were easier to preserve than low-contrast white source outputs.

reject_patterns.L086_industrial_studio_storage_scene: Industrial workshop reference contained shelves/racks; model merged scene shelving logic into the product, causing proportion and product appearance drift.
reject_patterns.L088_cafe_dessert_scene: Cafe dessert-table prompt plus white low-contrast source made the model simplify the offset fruit rack into a generic dessert stand and lose the middle vertical support.
reject_patterns.L088_coastal_breakfast_counter_scene: Scene became dominant and product geometry drifted into a different display tower/rack; outline changed despite good lifestyle atmosphere.

updated_rules: Do not keep images only because layout and differentiation are good; product hardware must pass separately.
Avoid structurally similar style references for high-structure products unless product scale is larger and hardware locks are stricter.
For L088, freeze offset stepped basket layout, central/vertical support rods, side rods, feet, connectors, and color; not a generic three-tier dessert stand.

For L086, avoid industrial shelf/workshop references with many shelving objects; preserve two drawer/basket units, front grid/transparent drawer face, top board, vertical supports, side frame, legs, and proportions.
Prefer high-contrast product PNGs or product inset when thin rods/supports are important.
